crossovers.description: Optional crossover configuration for multi-driver groups

recording_config.description: Recording configuration (device settings, signal parameters used during capture)

speakers.description: Map of channel name to speaker configuration

system.description: System configuration (v2.1) - Decouples logical roles from measurements

AreaPriorKind.description: Probability density shape over positions, JSON-serialisable.

oneOf.description: Uniform density over the configured `bounds`.

Axis-aligned Gaussian density. `mean` and `cov_diag` must each have
length equal to the number of dimensions; truncated at ±k·σ.

AreaQuadratureKind.description: Quadrature scheme for discretising the prior integral, JSON-serialisable.

oneOf.description: Sobol low-discrepancy QMC with `num_points` samples.

num_points.description: Number of quadrature points (powers of two are most efficient).

oneOf.description: Latin-Hypercube sampling.
Gauss–Legendre tensor product. Total points = `points_per_axis^D`.

AreaScalarisationKind.description: How to scalarise per-quadrature-point losses, JSON-serialisable.

oneOf.description: Probability-weighted mean (expected loss over the listening area).
Worst-case (max) over the area's bounding box. Inner DE search.
CVaR at level α — mean of the worst α-fraction of points.

AsymmetricLossConfig.description:
Configuration for asymmetric loss weighting.

Weights apply per sample as a multiplier on the squared error. The
peak / dip split uses a sigmoid crossfade in log frequency around
`transition_freq` so the transition from bass weighting to mid/treble
weighting is smooth. Narrow-null suppression is a separate mask passed
to the loss function at call time (see
[`crate::roomeq::impulse_analysis::build_null_suppression_mask`]).

bass_dip_weight.description:
Weight for bass dips (below `transition_freq`). Default: 1.0

Historically this defaulted to 0.2 (near-ignore) as a crude
proxy for "do not fight acoustic nulls". With explicit narrow-null
suppression in place, broad bass dips (SBIR, baffle step) become
legitimate correction targets and the dip weight is aligned with
the mid/treble default.

dip_weight.description: Weight for negative errors (dips above `transition_freq`). Default: 1.0

peak_weight.description: Weight for positive errors (peaks above `transition_freq`). Default: 2.0

transition_freq.description: Transition frequency between bass and mid/treble weighting. Default: 300.0 Hz

AudibilityDeadbandConfig.description:
Audibility/JND deadband applied to spectral objective residuals after
perceptual or octave smoothing.

disable_below_schroeder.description: Do not apply the deadband below the resolved Schroeder/modal region.

schroeder_hz.description: Schroeder fallback used when no measured value is available.

AutoOptimizerConfig.description: Opt-in automatic room EQ optimizer bound selection.

enabled.description: Enable automatic selection for the fields below.

filter_count.description: Automatically choose the maximum PEQ filter count.

gain_bounds.description: Automatically choose gain bounds and boost envelopes.

max_filters.description: Maximum filter count when automatic count selection is enabled.

min_filters.description: Minimum filter count when automatic count selection is enabled.

bass_anchor_coherence.description:
Magnitude-squared coherence γ² ∈ [0, 1] between the mic and the
loopback per-window phasors at `bass_freq_hz`. Conventional QA
threshold is γ² > 0.9. `None` when no loopback channel was
recorded.

bass_anchor_loopback_phase_deg.description:
Raw loopback reference phase in degrees (sin-referenced).
`None` when no loopback channel was recorded.

bass_anchor_magnitude.description:
Linear magnitude of the lock-in I/Q estimator at
`bass_freq_hz` on the mic. SNR proxy.

bass_anchor_phase_deg.description:
Reported phase of the bass tone at the listening position,
degrees in `(−180°, 180°]`, sin-referenced. When a loopback
reference channel was recorded this is the loopback-corrected
acoustic phase (`phase_mic − phase_loopback`); see
`bass_anchor_loopback_phase_deg` for the raw loopback value.

bass_anchor_stability_deg.description:
Circular standard deviation of phase across the sub-window
lock-in estimates, in degrees. Values above the
`"bass_anchor_unreliable"` advisory threshold (§2.8, 20°) mean
the GD confidence gate should discard this channel's anchor.

channel_index.description: Channel output index used during playback.

BassAnchorResultsLegacy.description:
Serializable mirror of the engine's `BassAnchorResults`. Captures
the per-channel phase at the bass anchor frequency so that GD-Opt
v2's confidence gate (§3.5 of `docs/gd_opt_v2_plan.md`) and
optimiser (§3.2) can ingest it at config-load time without
depending on `sotf-engine`.

Implements a custom `Deserialize` that also accepts the pre-v2
schema field name `bass_cycles: u16`; when present (and
`bass_duration_s` is absent) it is converted to seconds via
`cycles / bass_freq_hz` so older recordings.json files load without
re-recording.

bass_duration_s.description: Total tone length in seconds (steady portion + fades). Nominal 2.0.

bass_freq_hz.description: Centre frequency of the steady-state tone in Hz (nominal 30 Hz).

sample_rate.description: Sample rate used for the capture (Hz).

BassManagementConfig.description:
Home-cinema bass-management policy.

This describes how RoomEQ should reason about the main high-pass, sub
low-pass, redirected bass, and the LFE programme path. The current DSP
exporter still emits per-output-channel correction chains, so LFE +10 dB
is reported but not inserted by default: applying it to the physical sub
chain would also boost redirected bass.

apply_lfe_gain_to_chain.description:
Explicitly insert LFE gain in the exported physical sub chain. This is
normally false because redirected bass and LFE share the same sub output.

enabled.description: Enable bass-management semantics for home-cinema workflows.

group_crossovers.description:
Optional role-group to crossover-key mapping. Supported built-in group
ids are `lcr`, `surround`, `height`, and `wide`; unknown ids are kept as
custom metadata groups.

headroom_margin_db.description: Headroom reserve expected downstream for bass-managed playback.

headroom_model.description: Programme-correlation model used for bass-bus headroom simulation.

lfe_playback_gain_db.description:
Cinema LFE playback calibration gain. Reported in metadata; not applied
to the sub correction chain unless `apply_lfe_gain_to_chain` is set.

max_sub_boost_db.description: Maximum allowed positive sub boost from RoomEQ bass management.

optimize_groups.description: Optimize crossover settings independently per speaker group.

redirect_bass.description: Whether small-speaker bass is redirected to the subwoofer output.

sub_trim_db.description: User sub trim applied after crossover/alignment optimization.

BootstrapScalarisation.description: How to scalarise the per-bootstrap-resample losses into one outer-loop loss.

oneOf.description:
Pure worst-case: max loss across the B resamples. Most conservative; can be
driven by a single outlier resample.

Mean of the worst α-fraction of resamples (CVaR). Smoother, less sensitive
to a single freak resample than `WorstCase`.

BootstrapUncertaintyConfig.description:
Serializable bootstrap uncertainty configuration for JSON config files.

Drives `MultiMeasurementStrategy::MinimaxUncertainty`. At optimizer-setup
time, the input N measurement curves are case-bootstrap resampled B times;
each resampled mean becomes its own per-measurement objective. The outer
optimizer then scalarises the B objectives per `scalarisation`.

alpha.description: Two-sided confidence level α; band covers `[α/2, 1-α/2]`. Default: 0.10.

cvar_alpha.description: Tail fraction for CVaR (only used when `scalarisation = Cvar`). Default 0.20.

num_resamples.description: Number of bootstrap resamples B. Typical: 200..1000. Default: 400.

CardioidConfig.description: Configuration for Gradient Cardioid Subwoofer (2 subwoofers)

separation_meters.description: Physical separation distance in meters (between acoustic centers)

correction_mode.description: Correction mode: flat, score (unsupported in roomeq), auto (distance-aware flat)

listening_distance_m.description: Manual listening distance override in meters

min_freq.description: Override minimum correction frequency in Hz (Schroeder frequency)

nearfield_threshold_m.description: Distance threshold in meters for auto-mode diagnostics (default: 2.0m)

num_filters.description: Number of PEQ filters for speaker correction (default: 5)

speaker_name.description: Speaker name on spinorama.org (overrides speaker_name from MeasurementSource)

system_latency_ms.description: System round-trip latency in ms (for distance computation from impulse response)

Cea2034CorrectionMode.description: Correction mode for CEA2034 speaker pre-correction

oneOf.description: Correct Listening Window toward flat (best for nearfield <2m)
Unsupported in roomeq; Harman speaker score is anechoic-only
Auto-select the supported roomeq pre-correction from listening distance

max_filters.description: Maximum number of additional PEQ filters per channel for matching

threshold_db.description: ICD RMS threshold in dB below which no correction is applied

ContinuousListeningAreaConfig.description:
Serializable continuous listening-area configuration for JSON config files.

Drives `MultiSeatStrategy::ContinuousArea`. The optimizer integrates the
per-position objective over a continuous prior π(p) defined over a
`dimensions`-dimensional axis-aligned box, replacing the discrete seats
array with a continuous probability density.

`bounds.len()` must equal `dimensions`. For Gaussian priors,
`mean.len()` and `cov_diag.len()` must also equal `dimensions`.
`seat_positions.len()` must equal the number of discrete seats in the
calibration `MultiSeatMeasurements` and each row's length must equal
`dimensions`.

bounds.description:
Per-axis bounding-box bounds `(lo, hi)`. Always required; even for
Gaussian priors the bounds determine the truncation rectangle.

dimensions.description:
Number of spatial dimensions (typical: 1 for a couch line, 2 for an
MLP rectangle, 3 for a head-volume sweep). Currently 1, 2, and 3 are
supported by the runtime dispatcher.

idw_power.description: IDW power exponent for spatial interpolation (default 2.0).

scalarisation.description: How to scalarise the Q per-point losses.

seat_positions.description:
Spatial coordinates of each calibration seat in
`MultiSeatMeasurements`. Outer length = number of seats, inner length =
`dimensions`. Order must match the seat index in the measurements.

frequencies.description: Crossover frequencies in Hz (for 3-way and above)

include_room_eq_dsp.description:
Include the exported per-channel RoomEQ gain/EQ/delay response in the
acoustic plant before solving the CTC matrix. This matches the runtime
order where the global XTC matrix feeds the per-channel correction
chains.

reference_sweep.description: Optional emitted sweep WAV used for raw sweep deconvolution.

sweep_duration_s.description: Sweep duration in seconds, used to suppress log-sweep harmonic residues.

loopback.description: Raw loopback/reference recording WAV used to align the take.

raw_sweep.description: Raw recorded two-ear sweep WAV. Channel 1 = left ear, channel 2 = right ear.

DecomposedCorrectionSerdeConfig.description: Serializable decomposed correction configuration for JSON config files

early_reflection_weight.description: Correction weight for early reflections (0.0-1.0). Default: 0.3

enabled.description: Whether decomposed correction is enabled. Default: true

fdw_cycles.description: FDW window length in cycles before min/max clamping. Default: 8.0

fdw_enabled.description: Enable Frequency-Dependent Windowing when `ssir_wav_path` provides an IR.

fdw_max_window_ms.description: Maximum FDW window length in milliseconds. Default: 500.0

fdw_min_window_ms.description: Minimum FDW window length in milliseconds. Default: 3.0

fdw_smoothing_octaves.description: FDW smoothing width in octaves. Default: 1/24 octave

min_mode_prominence_db.description: Minimum prominence (dB) for mode detection. Default: 3.0

min_mode_q.description: Minimum Q to qualify as a room mode. Default: 3.0

mode_correction_weight.description: Correction weight for detected room modes (0.0-1.0). Default: 1.0

room_dimensions.description:
Room dimensions (L × W × H in metres). When present together with
a measured impulse response, enables a measurement-driven
Schroeder frequency via `RoomDimensions::schroeder_frequency_with_rt60`
using the RT60 measured from the IR. When absent, the optimizer
falls back to the `schroeder_freq` field above.

schroeder_freq.description:
Schroeder frequency (Hz). Below: modal, above: statistical.

When `room_dimensions` is also provided AND an impulse response is
available, this value is overridden at run time by a
measurement-driven Schroeder frequency: the optimizer measures
RT60 from the IR via Schroeder backward integration and plugs it
into `f_S ≈ 2000 · √(RT60 / V)` with V from `room_dimensions`. In
that case this field is used only as the fallback if the RT60 fit
fails.

steady_state_weight.description: Correction weight for steady-state above Schroeder (0.0-1.0). Default: 0.4

EarlyLateCorrectionConfig.description:
Direct/early/late impulse-response reporting windows for FIR and
mixed-phase modes.

direct_window_ms.description: End of the direct-sound window in milliseconds.

early_cue_risk_db.description: Advisory threshold for direct/early correction energy.

early_window_ms.description: End of the direct-plus-early window in milliseconds.

late_window_ms.description: End of the late-reflection summary window in milliseconds.

evaluation_weight.description: Weights for the three EPA dimensions in the composite score

flatness_band_weight.description:
Band weight for the flatness component of the EPA loss.
Default 0.0 (see `flatness_erb_weight`).

flatness_band_weights.description:
Band weights used for the flatness component of the EPA loss.
Only consulted when `flatness_band_weight > 0`.

flatness_erb_weight.description:
ERB weight for the flatness component of the EPA loss.
Default 1.0 (pure ERB) because EPA already has band-sensitive
sharpness / roughness / loudness_balance terms — adding band
weighting on top of flatness would double-count frequency bias.

listening_level_phon.description: Listening level in phon (affects loudness computation)

max_roughness.description: Maximum acceptable roughness (above this, penalty increases)

target_sharpness.description: Target sharpness in acum (1.0 = natural broadband noise character)

temporal_masking.description:
Temporal-masking penalties for modal ringing and FIR phase behavior.

Modal data is used as an optimizer-cheap proxy for post-masked room
decay audibility. When FIR coefficients are exported, the FIR impulse
response is also analyzed directly for pre/post ringing audibility.

f3_reference_max_hz.description: Upper bound of the reference band used for F3 auto-detection.

f3_reference_min_hz.description: Lower bound of the reference band used for F3 auto-detection.

manual_f3_hz.description: Manual F3 override in Hz (used if auto_detect_f3 is false)

margin_octaves.description: Safety margin in octaves below F3 for HPF placement

correct_excess_phase.description: Whether to correct excess phase (only applies to kirkeby mode)

phase_smoothing.description: Phase smoothing width in octaves (default: 0.167 = 1/6 octave)

FrequencyBandWeights.description: Frequency band configuration for weighted loss

bass_weight.description: Weight for bass band (default: 2.0 - bass is more critical for room correction)

treble_weight.description: Weight for treble band (default: 0.8 - less critical for room issues)

adaptive_allpass.description: Require adaptive AP bootstrap before emitting all-pass filters.

ap_per_channel.description:
Maximum all-pass filters per channel. Production may downgrade this to
zero when bootstrap evidence is unavailable.

coherence_threshold.description: Minimum in-band mean coherence for polarity/AP optimization.

enabled.description: Enable GD optimization and apply the resulting phase-only DSP.

min_improvement_db.description: Minimum improvement required before applying GD DSP.

optimize_polarity.description: Whether polarity may be optimized when coherence is present.

HighFreqFilterConfig.description: High frequency filter configuration for Schroeder split

max_q.description: Maximum Q factor for high frequency filters (tone controls only)

HighFrequencyCorrectionConfig.description:
Safer high-frequency correction behavior when users opt above the
conservative RoomEQ correction ceiling.

max_q.description:
If the global Q bound is still at/above this value, cap it when the
policy is applied. Frequency-selective Q caps are not available in the
current PEQ parameterization, so this is intentionally conservative.

smoothing_n.description:
Stronger psychoacoustic smoothing resolution above the guard start,
expressed as 1/N octave. Lower N means broader smoothing.

start_hz.description: Frequency above which the safeguard is considered active.

HighpassType.description: Highpass filter type for excursion protection

oneOf.description: Linkwitz-Riley (4th order = 24dB/oct)
Butterworth

csv_path.description: Optional path to associated CSV file

wav_path.description: Optional path to associated WAV file

LowFreqFilterConfig.description: Low frequency filter configuration for Schroeder split

max_db.description: Maximum boost/cut in dB for below-Schroeder filters

max_q.description: Maximum Q factor for low frequency filters (allow high-Q for modes)

anyOf.description: Inline measurement data (stored directly in JSON)

name.description: Optional display name for the measurement.

path.description: Path to the CSV measurement file.

anyOf.description: Path to CSV file (freq, spl, phase columns)

MeasurementSingle.description:
Single measurement with metadata

Custom implementation to support both string path and object with speaker_name

MeasurementSource.description: Source of measurements (single file, multiple files for averaging, or in-memory curve)

anyOf.description: A single measurement file with optional speaker name
Multiple measurement files to be averaged with optional speaker name

MixedModeConfig.description: Configuration for frequency-based mixed mode crossover

crossover_freq.description: Crossover frequency dividing IIR and FIR bands (Hz)

MixedPhaseSerdeConfig.description: Serializable mixed-phase correction configuration for JSON config files

max_fir_length_ms.description: Maximum FIR length in milliseconds for excess phase correction. Default: 10.0

min_spatial_depth.description: Minimum spatial correction depth for excess phase correction. Default: 0.5

phase_smoothing_octaves.description: Phase smoothing width in octaves. Default: 1/6 octave

bootstrap_uncertainty.description: Bootstrap uncertainty configuration (used when strategy = MinimaxUncertainty).

spatial_robustness.description: Spatial robustness configuration (used when strategy = SpatialRobustness)

variance_lambda.description: Lambda for VariancePenalized (default 1.0). Higher = more consistent across positions.

weights.description: Weights for WeightedSum (normalized internally). Equal if omitted.

MultiMeasurementStrategy.description: Strategy for handling multiple measurements per speaker

oneOf.description: RMS-average curves, optimize on average (existing behavior)
loss = Σ w_i * loss_i — weighted sum of per-measurement losses
loss = max(loss_i) — optimize worst case across all measurements
loss = mean(loss_i) + λ * var(loss_i) — balance quality + consistency

Spatial robustness: RMS-average + correction depth mask based on spatial variance.
Only corrects features consistent across positions.

Measurement-uncertainty-aware robust optimization. Generates B
case-bootstrap resamples of the input curves at setup time, then
scalarises losses across the resampled targets per the configured
`BootstrapUncertaintyConfig::scalarisation` (worst-case or CVaR).
Drives the optimizer toward a solution that is robust to which
resample of the measurement set is "true".

all_channel_enabled.description: Enable all-channel multi-seat correction for non-sub home-cinema channels.

all_channel_strategy.description: Strategy used when deriving per-channel multi-measurement correction.

allpass_filters_per_sub.description: Number of per-sub all-pass filters allowed during MSO.

continuous_area.description:
Continuous listening-area prior. Required (and only consulted) when
`strategy = ContinuousArea`.

global_eq.description: Optimize a shared EQ on the post-MSO combined response across all seats.

max_deviation_db.description: Maximum allowed deviation at non-primary seats (dB)

optimize_polarity.description: Enable per-sub polarity search for MSO.

per_sub_peq.description:
Optimize a per-subwoofer PEQ from that sub's measurements across all seats
before the gain/delay/polarity/all-pass MSO pass.

primary_seat.description: Index of primary seat (0-based, used with PrimaryWithConstraints strategy)

primary_seat_weight.description: Relative primary-seat weight used with PrimaryWithConstraints.

seat_weights.description: Optional seat weights for all-channel multi-seat correction.

oneOf.description: Minimize standard deviation across all seats (default)
Optimize for primary seat with constraints on others
Optimize for average response across all seats
Complex modal-basis sound-field management across seats

Continuous listening-area prior: integrate the variance / mean / worst-case
objective over a probability density over positions, instead of the
discrete seat slots. Requires `MultiSeatConfig::continuous_area` to be set.

asymmetric_loss.description: Enable asymmetric loss (peaks penalized 2x more than dips)

asymmetric_loss_config.description: Asymmetric loss weights. `None` preserves the built-in default.

audibility_deadband.description: Audibility/JND deadband for spectral residual objectives.

auto_optimizer.description: Automatic selection of filter count and optimizer bounds.

bo_batch_size.description: Bayesian optimization batch size. `None` uses the backend default.

bo_ehvi.description: Use Monte-Carlo qEHVI Bayesian optimization for multi-objective data.

bo_initial_samples.description: Bayesian optimization Sobol hot-start samples. `None` uses an automatic default.

bo_posterior_std_threshold.description: Posterior standard-deviation threshold for BO local-refiner handoff.

epa_config.description:
EPA loss configuration. Only used when `loss_type == "epa"`.
When `None`, the optimizer falls back to
[`EpaConfig::default`](crate::loss::epa::score::EpaConfig::default).

fir.description: FIR configuration (used when `processing_mode` requires FIR filters)

group_delay.description: Group-delay optimization configuration. Disabled by default.

high_frequency_correction.description: Safeguards for high-frequency correction above the conservative range.

local_algo.description: Local optimizer algorithm for refinement stage

loss_type.description:
Loss function type. Supported values:
- `"flat"` — minimize deviation from target (default)
- `"score"` — maximize Harman/Olive preference score
- `"epa"` — EPA (Evaluation/Potency/Activity) psychoacoustic
  loss combining spectral flatness with sharpness, roughness,
  and loudness-balance penalties derived from Zwicker metrics.
  When selected, the EPA penalty weights can be customized via
  the [`epa_config`](Self::epa_config) field; otherwise the
  defaults from [`EpaConfig::default`](crate::loss::epa::score::EpaConfig::default)
  are used.

max_boost_envelope.description:
Frequency-dependent maximum boost envelope.
Each entry is (frequency_hz, max_boost_db).
Between points, linear interpolation in log-frequency.
Default: None (use the existing flat `max_db` limit).
When set, overrides `max_db` on a per-frequency basis.

min_cut_envelope.description:
CDT-aware minimum cut envelope: limits how deep the optimizer can cut
at frequencies where the ear generates Cubic Distortion Tones.
Each entry is (frequency_hz, max_cut_db) where max_cut_db is negative.
Default: None (no CDT protection).

min_filter_improvement.description: Minimum loss improvement to justify adding another filter

num_filters.description: Maximum number of PEQ filters per channel

perceptual_policy.description:
Product-level perceptual policy preset. When omitted, legacy optimizer
defaults are preserved.

phase_alignment.description: Phase alignment configuration for subwoofer integration

processing_mode.description: Processing mode — selects the filter class used for correction.

psychoacoustic_smoothing.description: Psychoacoustic variable-smoothing settings. `None` preserves the built-in default.

refine.description: Whether to run local refinement after global optimization

seed.description: Random seed for reproducible results (None for random)

smooth_n.description: Loss function smoothing resolution as 1/N octave

smoothness_penalty.description: Smoothness regularizer on the correction curve.

target_response.description: Unified target response configuration (shape + preference shelves + broadband pre-correction)

PerceptualPolicyConfig.description: Perceptual policy selector layered above the detailed optimizer settings.

apply_defaults.description: Fill policy-derived defaults into lower-level settings.

override_existing.description:
Override explicit lower-level settings instead of only filling missing
optional fields and primitive values that are still at their defaults.

PerceptualPolicyPreset.description:
Product-level perceptual policy preset.

Policies fill in coherent defaults for the lower-level RoomEQ knobs. Omitted
policies preserve the historical optimizer defaults.

oneOf.description: Conservative reference correction with strong early-cue preservation.
Music listening target with broad in-room tilt and robust LF correction.
Home-cinema target policy with role-aware dialog and X-curve shaping.
Quiet-listening policy with headroom/bass restraint and dialog emphasis.
Speech-intelligibility policy with stronger dialog-band correction.

PhaseAlignmentConfig.description: Phase alignment configuration for subwoofer integration

PreRingingSerdeConfig.description: Serializable pre-ringing configuration for JSON config files

max_time_s.description: Maximum pre-ringing time in seconds. Default: 0.005 (5 ms)

threshold_db.description: Maximum pre-ringing level in dB relative to main tap. Default: -30.0

high_hz.description: Upper edge of the band in Hz (inclusive).

limit_db.description: Maximum lagging-source level above the primary in this band, in dB.

low_hz.description: Lower edge of the band in Hz (inclusive).

ProbeResultsLegacy.description:
Serializable mirror of the engine's `ProbeDelayResults`. Kept
in-crate (rather than depending on `sotf-engine` or `sotf-player`)
so the autoeq crate remains lean. Fields match the engine type
1:1 so round-trip through serde is lossless.

ProcessingMode.description: Processing mode for the optimization engine

oneOf.description: Low-latency mode (IIR filters only) - < 5ms latency
Phase-linear mode (FIR filters only) - High latency allowed
Hybrid mode (IIR for bass, FIR for mids/highs) - Variable latency

Mixed-phase mode (IIR for minimum-phase + excess phase FIR)
Requires phase data in measurements. Low latency (~10ms).

Warped IIR mode — exports RoomEQ filters as warped biquads using a
Bark-scale lambda. The optimizer currently uses the same biquad
placement/scoring path as low_latency, then serializes the runtime
topology as `warped_biquad`.

Kautz modal mode — pole-tuned filter targeting detected room modes.
Uses room mode analysis to place filter poles at resonance frequencies.
Gain optimization via linear least-squares (very fast, no DE needed).
Exports the runtime topology as `kautz_filter` with modal sections.
Best for small, highly resonant rooms with clear modal problems.
Returns an error if no room modes are detected.

PsychoacousticSmoothingConfig.description:
Psychoacoustic variable smoothing configuration

Different frequency ranges benefit from different smoothing levels:
- Low frequencies (< 100 Hz): Fine resolution (1/48 octave) to preserve room modes
- High frequencies (> 1 kHz): Coarse resolution (1/6 octave) to ignore comb filtering
- Transition region (100 Hz - 1 kHz): Gradual interpolation between the two

high_freq.description: Upper transition frequency in Hz (default: 1000 Hz)

high_freq_n.description: Smoothing resolution above high_freq (bands per octave, e.g., 6 for 1/6 octave)

low_freq.description: Lower transition frequency in Hz (default: 100 Hz)

low_freq_n.description: Smoothing resolution below low_freq (bands per octave, e.g., 48 for 1/48 octave)

RecordingConfiguration.description:
Recording configuration stored with measurements
Contains device settings and signal parameters used during measurement capture

bass_anchor_results.description:
Bass anchor results captured during the GD-1e BassAnchor
wizard step — per-channel phase of a low-frequency tone burst
at `bass_probe_freq_hz`. Populated after the wizard finishes;
absent when the user skipped the step.

bass_anchor_wav_relative.description:
Relative path (within the recording directory) of the raw
bass-anchor WAV. `None` when the BassAnchor step was skipped
or when recording persistence was disabled.

bass_octave_duration_s.description:
Per-octave bass sweep duration in seconds. Defaults to 3.0; the
sweep generator scales total duration so that the band below
100 Hz receives `bass_octave_duration_s` seconds per octave.
Clamped to `[1.0 .. 10.0]` at load time.

bass_probe_duration_s.description:
Total length of the steady-state bass-anchor tone in seconds
(steady portion + fades). Default 2.0.

The pre-v2 schema stored `bass_probe_cycles: u16` here — it's
dropped silently at load (the units differ and converting
requires `bass_probe_freq_hz` which serde sees per-field). The
per-result `BassAnchorResultsLegacy::bass_duration_s` migration
handles legacy run-output files (where the conversion is well
defined).

bass_probe_freq_hz.description:
Centre frequency of the bass tone burst captured by the
BassAnchor wizard step. Default 20.0 Hz (or
`1.25 * min_freq`, whichever is higher).

channel_speakers.description:
Per-channel speaker identity (brand + model) as free-form
strings, ideally autocompleted from the spinorama.org catalog.
Keyed by channel name so it round-trips through reorder/rename.
Not consumed by the optimizer — metadata only.

coherence_threshold.description:
Coherence threshold below which the GD confidence gate declares
bass phase untrustworthy. Default 0.9. Clamped to `[0.5 .. 0.99]`.

mic_phase_calibration_path.description:
Path to the microphone phase calibration CSV (4 columns:
`freq, mag_db, phase_deg, coherence`). Magnitude calibration
already lives under [`mic_calibration_path`](Self::mic_calibration_path).

mic_phase_calibration_paths.description:
Per-channel mic phase calibration files, aligned with
[`mic_calibration_paths`](Self::mic_calibration_paths).

num_positions.description:
Number of measurement positions (seats) the user captured. `None`
or `Some(1)` means a single-position session; `Some(n)` with
`n >= 2` means each `ChannelMeasurement.multi_mic_measurements`
holds `n * num_mics - 1` entries in `(position, mic)` order
(primary measurement is `(0, 0)`).

num_sweeps.description:
Number of sweeps recorded back-to-back for coherence averaging.
Default 4. Clamped to `[1 .. 8]` at load time.

post_silence_s.description:
Post-sweep silence window in seconds. Default
`schroeder_rt60 + 1.0`; falls back to `2.0` if no RT60 estimate
is available.

pre_silence_s.description:
Pre-sweep silence window in seconds. Used by the coherence
averager to estimate the noise-floor. Default 2.0.

probe_results.description:
Tone-burst delay probe results captured during the Recording
wizard's Probe step. Stored here so the `autoeq::roomeq`
pipeline can pick them up at config-load time without requiring
a live measurement. Mirrors the shape of the engine's
`ProbeDelayResults` for cross-crate serde compatibility.

probe_wav_relative.description:
Relative path (within the recording directory) of the raw
probe WAV persisted by `probe_channel_delays_with_recording`.
`None` for sessions that skipped the Probe step.

room_dimensions.description:
Physical room dimensions (metric — length/width/height in meters)
collected from the user at save time. When present these are
reused by the optimizer's Schroeder-frequency auto-detection; see
[`RoomDimensions::schroeder_frequency_with_rt60`].

setup_description.description:
Free-form description of the listening setup (treatment,
seating, notes about speaker placement, etc.). Not consumed by
the optimizer — stored purely for session reproducibility.

signal_type.description: Signal type used for measurements (e.g. "Sweep", "Pink Noise")

spl_calibration.description:
SPL calibration anchor captured from a pre-sweep reference tone.
Required on new recordings by the SplCalibration wizard step;
stored here so that replayed recordings can re-derive
`sweep_level_db_spl`.

sweep_end_freq.description: Sweep end frequency in Hz (only applicable when signal_type is "Sweep")

sweep_level_db_spl.description:
Target sweep level at the listening position in dBSPL. Requires
[`spl_calibration`](Self::spl_calibration) to be populated.

sweep_start_freq.description: Sweep start frequency in Hz (only applicable when signal_type is "Sweep")

RoleTargetConfig.description:
Optional role-aware target adjustments for home-cinema layouts.

These are deliberately explicit and default to zero change: enabling the
block makes the target semantics role-aware without silently changing
existing RoomEQ output. The adjustments layer on top of
[`TargetResponseConfig::preference`].

center_dialog_boost_db.description: Broad, smooth center-channel dialog-band target lift/cut in dB.

center_dialog_high_hz.description: Upper edge of the center dialog emphasis band.

center_dialog_low_hz.description: Lower edge of the center dialog emphasis band.

center_slope_offset_db_per_octave.description: Extra broadband slope for the center channel, in dB/octave.

center_treble_shelf_db.description: Additional treble shelf applied only to the centre channel.

cinema_reference_distance_m.description: Reference distance for distance-compensated treble rolloff.

cinema_x_curve_db_per_octave.description: Additional high-frequency slope above `cinema_x_curve_start_hz`, in dB/octave.

cinema_x_curve_enabled.description: Enable cinema/X-curve style high-frequency rolloff shaping.

cinema_x_curve_start_hz.description: Frequency where cinema/X-curve high-frequency shaping starts.

distance_treble_rolloff_db_per_doubling.description: Additional HF rolloff per distance doubling beyond the reference distance.

front_slope_offset_db_per_octave.description: Extra broadband slope for front L/R channels, in dB/octave.

height_slope_offset_db_per_octave.description: Extra broadband slope for height channels, in dB/octave.

height_treble_shelf_db.description: Additional treble shelf applied to height channels.

lfe_bass_shelf_db.description: Additional bass shelf applied to LFE channels.

lfe_slope_offset_db_per_octave.description: Extra broadband slope for LFE channels, in dB/octave.

listening_distance_m.description: Listening distance used for optional distance-compensated treble rolloff.

subwoofer_bass_shelf_db.description: Additional bass shelf applied to subwoofer channels.

subwoofer_slope_offset_db_per_octave.description: Extra broadband slope for subwoofer channels, in dB/octave.

surround_slope_offset_db_per_octave.description: Extra broadband slope for surround and wide channels, in dB/octave.

surround_treble_shelf_db.description: Additional treble shelf applied to side/rear/wide surrounds.

RoomDimensions.description: Room dimensions for automatic Schroeder frequency calculation

SmoothnessPenaltyConfigSerde.description: Serializable smoothness-penalty configuration for JSON config files.

exponent.description: L_p exponent. 1.0 = TV^2-like sparse curvature, 2.0 = L2 smoothing.

schroeder_hz.description: Optional Schroeder cutoff in Hz for reduced modal-region penalty.

tv2_weight.description: Penalty weight in loss units per (dB/decade^2)^exponent.

SpatialRobustnessSerdeConfig.description: Serializable spatial robustness configuration for JSON config files

mask_smoothing_octaves.description: Smoothing width in octaves for the correction depth mask. Default: 1/6 octave.

transition_width_db.description: Transition width (dB) for sigmoid blending. Default: 2.0

variance_threshold_db.description: Variance threshold (dB) below which full correction is allowed. Default: 3.0

SpeakerConfig.description:
Speaker configuration (can be single measurement or group)

Variant order matters for serde untagged deserialization: serde tries each variant
in order. `SupportingSource` is first because it requires the unique `primary` and
`support` fields. Group/MultiSub/Dba all require a `name` field that `MeasurementSource`
doesn't have, so they are tried before `Single`. `Single` is last as a catch-all.

anyOf.description: Supporting-source room compensation (primary + delayed support loudspeaker).

SpeakerGroup.description: Group of measurements for a single speaker (multi-driver)

SplCalibration.description:
SPL calibration anchor captured from a pre-sweep reference tone.

Maps the peak sample value observed on the recording ADC during a
reference tone to a dBSPL reading taken by the user at the listening
position with an external meter. Once captured, the sweep playback
level is chosen so the in-band energy at the listening position hits
[`RecordingConfiguration::sweep_level_db_spl`](RecordingConfiguration::sweep_level_db_spl)
deterministically — avoiding the subwoofer driver over-excursion
that would otherwise contaminate bass phase with harmonic distortion.

peak_sample_level.description:
Peak sample value observed on the recording ADC during the tone,
in the range `[0.0 .. 1.0]`.

reference_freq_hz.description: Frequency of the calibration tone in Hz (default 1000).

reported_db_spl.description: User-reported dBSPL at the listening position during calibration.

spl_offset_db.description:
Offset mapping ADC peak level to dBSPL at the mic:
`dbspl_at_mic = 20 * log10(peak_sample_value) + spl_offset_db`.

max_db.description: Maximum boost in dB (room gain can be 15+ dB at resonances)

max_q.description: Maximum Q factor (higher Q for narrow room modes)

num_filters.description: Number of PEQ filters for subwoofer channels

oneOf.description: Single subwoofer optimization (default)
Multi-Sub Optimizer (minimize seat-to-seat variance)
Double Bass Array (active cancellation)

crossover.description: Crossover reference key (points to entry in `crossovers` map)

SupportingSourceConfig.description: Configuration for a single supporting-source loudspeaker channel.

decorrelation.description: Decorrelator applied to the supporting source path.

delay_ms.description: Delay of the supporting source relative to the primary at the listening position.

fir_taps.description: FIR length in taps. Default follows Brooks-Park (≈186 ms at 44.1 kHz).

freq_range_hz.description: Compensation band. Outside this band the supporting source is muted.

precedence_limits.description: Frequency-dependent precedence limits. The first matching band wins.

target_response.description: Target response to match. Defaults to the room-level `target_curve`.

velvet_noise_taps.description: Velvet-noise sequence length when decorrelation is enabled.

SupportingSourceDecorrelation.description: Decorrelation strategy for the supporting source path.

oneOf.description: Apply a velvet-noise sequence to the supporting source.
No decorrelation.

SupportingSourceGroup.description:
A speaker group representing a primary loudspeaker plus a delayed,
decorrelated supporting loudspeaker for room compensation.

This variant is distinguished from other `SpeakerConfig` variants by the
presence of the `primary` and `support` fields.

name.description: Human-readable name for this primary/support pair.

primary.description: Measurement source for the primary loudspeaker.

support.description: Measurement source for the supporting loudspeaker.

SupportingSourceOutputNaming.description: Naming convention for supporting-source physical outputs.

suffix.description:
Suffix appended to the logical role for the supporting output.
Default: "_support".

SystemConfig.description: Explicit system configuration mapping logical roles to measurements

speakers.description: Map of logical role to measurement key

supporting_source_outputs.description: Naming convention for supporting-source physical outputs.

anyOf.description: Path to CSV file (freq, spl columns)

TargetResponseConfig.description: Unified target response configuration for room correction

broadband_precorrection.description: Enable broadband pre-correction (shelf+gain fit before fine EQ)

curve_path.description: Path to custom target curve CSV (used when shape == File)

preference.description: User preference adjustments (layered ON TOP of the target shape)

reference_freq.description: Reference frequency where target shape equals 0 dB (Hz)

slope_db_per_octave.description: Slope in dB per octave (used when shape == Custom)

oneOf.description: Flat in-room response (no tilt)
Harman preferred in-room curve (-0.8 dB/octave from 1 kHz reference)
Custom slope specified by `slope_db_per_octave`
Load target curve from external CSV file (`curve_path` must be set)
Derive slope from the input measurement curve at optimization time

TemporalMaskingConfig.description: Temporal masking penalty configuration for EPA optimization.

enabled.description: Enable the temporal masking penalty when modal data is available.

ir_audibility_threshold_db.description:
Audibility floor for weighted pre/post ringing energy, in dB relative
to the main impulse peak.

ir_enabled.description:
Enable true FIR impulse-response pre/post masking analysis when FIR
coefficients are available.

ir_weight.description: Weight applied to the IR masking penalty metric.

post_mask_ms.description:
Post-masking window after the main impulse. Ringing grows more audible
as it decays beyond this window.

pre_mask_ms.description:
Pre-masking window before the main impulse. Pre-ringing inside this
window is partially masked; earlier energy is fully audible.

pre_ringing_weight.description:
Relative weight for audible pre-ringing. Usually higher than post
because pre-echo before a transient is especially objectionable.

profile.description: Material profile used to scale modal-ringing audibility.

weight.description: Weight applied to the normalized temporal masking penalty.

oneOf.description:
Percussive material: modal ringing is least masked and should be cut
more decisively.
General music / film content.
Sustained material: late modal decay is partly masked by ongoing tone.

UserPreference.description: User preference adjustments layered on top of the target shape

bass_shelf_db.description: Bass shelf boost/cut in dB (applied below `bass_shelf_freq`)

treble_shelf_db.description: Treble shelf boost/cut in dB (applied above `treble_shelf_freq`)

abx.description: Include ABX descriptors for before/after listening checks.

enabled.description: Generate a JSON validation bundle descriptor alongside RoomEQ output.

mushra.description: Include MUSHRA descriptors for multi-condition preference tests.

perceptual_regression_summary.description: Include perceptual regression summaries and advisories.

target_lufs.description: Loudness target for matched program-material assets.

VoiceOfGodConfig.description: Configuration for Voice of God (Timbre Matching)
